user.title: UTE Tarifas
user.description: Set up residential UTE tariff tracking. Prices and schedules are maintained in the integration and update automatically — leave schedule fields blank to use the built-in UTE defaults.

data_description.contract_type: The UTE residential plan you are subscribed to (Simple, Double, or Triple).
data_description.schedule_workday: Leave blank to use the built-in UTE workday schedule. Custom format: HH:MM-HH:MM:period,… (periods: valle, llano, punta, simple).
data_description.schedule_weekend: Leave blank to use the built-in UTE weekend schedule (all-llano on weekends for Double; all-valle for Triple).
data_description.schedule_holiday: Leave blank to use the built-in UTE holiday schedule (all-llano for Double; all-valle for Triple).
data_description.country: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for national holiday detection. Default: UY.
data_description.use_national_holidays: When enabled, national holidays follow the holiday schedule instead of the weekday schedule.
data_description.monthly_kwh: Used only for the Simple contract to determine the consumption tier (0–100, 101–600, or 601+ kWh/month). Ignored for Double/Triple contracts.

error.invalid_schedule: Invalid schedule format. Expected HH:MM-HH:MM:period,… — for example: 00:00-07:00:valle,07:00-17:00:punta,17:00-00:00:valle
error.invalid_country: Unsupported country code. Use a valid ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code (e.g. UY, AR, BR).

init.title: Update schedule overrides
init.description: Clear a field to revert to the built-in canonical UTE schedule for that day type.

data_description.schedule_workday: Leave blank to use the built-in UTE workday schedule.
data_description.schedule_weekend: Leave blank to use the built-in UTE weekend schedule.
data_description.schedule_holiday: Leave blank to use the built-in UTE holiday schedule.
data_description.monthly_kwh: Used only for the Simple contract to determine the consumption tier (0–100, 101–600, or 601+ kWh/month). Ignored for Double/Triple contracts.

error.invalid_schedule: Invalid schedule format. Expected HH:MM-HH:MM:period,… — for example: 00:00-07:00:valle,07:00-17:00:punta,17:00-00:00:valle
